calendar_tier: TIER 1 — NFL Draft Week (April 23-25, 2026). We are now in the intensive draft coverage window with 17 days until Day 1. This dominates the NFL content today.

tweet_volume_check.dominant_topic: College Basketball National Championship — UConn vs Michigan Monday night. Calendar says draft week but tweet volume shows March Madness finale still owns the conversation.

editorial_reasoning.lead_choice: UConn vs Michigan National Championship Preview — Final Four recap, injury updates (Solo Ball, Lendeborg), coaching contract extensions, Dusty May staying. Multiple angles, high tweet engagement, calendar event.

stories_considered_and_demoted.reason: Respectful career achievement but only news-breaker tweets, no reaction content. Small story treatment.
Continuation of yesterday's lead story. Would be repetitive. Skip entirely.
Good stat but limited tweet support. Around the League item.

stories_promoted.reason: Historic rookie achievement with great StatMuse content. ROY race angle.
Lakers star, playoff implications, horse semen meme potential from NBA Centel tweet.
Wholesome family moment, good ClutchPoints content, Warriors playoff push context.

editorial_reasoning.tweet_gaps: Need more reaction tweets for draft mock content — only have news-breaker tweets. May search for analyst/fan reactions to mock drafts.

stories.headline: UConn and Michigan Set for Monday's National Championship Clash
hooks: Solo Ball and Lendeborg injury updates
Dusty May staying at Michigan despite UNC interest
Dan Hurley going for back-to-back titles
Nate Oats contract extension amid coaching carousel

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: UConn attempting back-to-back championships, Michigan trying to break through
sub_angles.running_joke: Coaching carousel panic with everyone getting extensions
sub_angles.who_else_affected: North Carolina still searching, other coaches securing bags
sub_angles.cross_connection: Final Four in Indianapolis where Dusty May was once a student manager begging for jobs
sub_angles.where_it_goes: Championship legacy implications for both programs

research.context: Championship game Monday night, injury concerns, coaching stability
research.connections: Student manager story, North Carolina coaching search, contract extensions

blocks.hint: Hook — Monday night, UConn vs Michigan for the national championship. Frame as the culmination of March Madness chaos meeting blue blood expectations.

blocks.why: Official injury update on key players — both should be available

blocks.hint: Injury update pivot — Both Solo Ball (foot) and Lendeborg (knee/ankle) should play. No excuses Monday. Now it's about execution.

blocks.why: Dusty May staying at Michigan news-breaker tweet

blocks.hint: Dusty May staying angle — He's not chasing the UNC job. Michigan's run convinced him he's already where he needs to be. Frame as confidence vs. Carolina's desperation.

blocks.context: Coaching carousel intensifying as everyone locks up their guys

blocks.why: Nate Oats contract making him top 5 highest paid

blocks.hint: Kicker — The coaching market is insane. Nate Oats just became a top-5 paid coach. Everyone's locking up their guy before Carolina panics and throws $15 million at them.

stories.headline: Cooper Flagg Becomes First Rookie in 30 Years with Back-to-Back 40+ Point Games
hooks: Most points in two games since Wilt

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: 96 points in two games, first since Iverson 1997
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Kon Knueppel ROY race, betting odds shift
sub_angles.where_it_goes: Rookie of the Year award implications

research.context: ROY race tightening, historic rookie achievement
research.connections: Wilt records, Iverson parallels, betting odds

blocks.hint: Hook — Cooper Flagg just did something no rookie has done in 30 years. Back-to-back 40+ point games. The last guy? Allen Iverson in 1997.

blocks.why: The historic stat with Iverson comparison
96 points in two games, most since Wilt

blocks.hint: Wilt connection — 96 points in two games. The most by a rookie since Wilt. We're watching generational talent in real time.

blocks.context: Each stat more ridiculous than the last
blocks.avoid: Don't search basketball — search 'Vince McMahon progressive excitement'

stories.headline: Stephon Gilmore Retires After 12-Year Hall of Fame Career

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: 2019 Defensive Player of the Year, multiple All-Pro selections
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Patriots championship legacy, Belichick defensive schemes

blocks.hint: Stephon Gilmore announced his retirement after 12 seasons. 2019 Defensive Player of the Year. Canton-bound. Play it straight — this one earns respect.
